title: Analytical Geometry
blurb: Distance, midpoint, gradient, inclination, straight lines, plane shapes, circles and tangents.

questions.context: In the diagram, $\triangle ABC$ has vertices $A(-2; 1)$, $B(4; 5)$ and $C(6; 2)$.
questions.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 220" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><line x1="20" y1="164" x2="308" y2="164" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="116" y1="8" x2="116" y2="196" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1"/><text x="300" y="158" font-size="13" fill="currentColor">x</text><text x="122" y="18" font-size="13" fill="currentColor">y</text><text x="104" y="178" font-size="13" fill="currentColor">O</text><path d="M68 138 L212 34 L260 112 Z" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" fill="none"/><circle cx="68" cy="138" r="2.5" fill="currentColor"/><circle cx="212" cy="34" r="2.5" fill="currentColor"/><circle cx="260" cy="112" r="2.5" fill="currentColor"/><text x="24" y="152" font-size="13" fill="currentColor">A(-2;1)</text><text x="204" y="24" font-size="13" fill="currentColor">B(4;5)</text><text x="264" y="126" font-size="13" fill="currentColor">C(6;2)</text></svg>

parts.q: Calculate the length of $AB$. Leave your answer in simplest surd form.
Prove that $\triangle ABC$ is right-angled at $B$.
solution: $\therefore AB\perp BC$, so $\hat{B}=90^{\circ}$ and $\triangle ABC$ is right-angled at $B$.

parts.q: Hence calculate the area of $\triangle ABC$.

questions.context: The points $P(-1; 3)$ and $Q(4; -2)$ lie on a straight line in the Cartesian plane.

parts.q: Calculate the gradient of the line $PQ$.
Determine $\theta$, the angle of inclination of $PQ$.
solution: Since the gradient is negative, $\theta$ is obtuse: $\theta=180^{\circ}-45^{\circ}=135^{\circ}$

parts.q: Determine the equation of the line through $Q$ perpendicular to $PQ$. Write your answer in the form $y=mx+c$.

questions.context: In the diagram, $ABCD$ is a quadrilateral with vertices $A(-2; -3)$, $B(4; -1)$, $C(6; 4)$ and $D$. The diagonals $AC$ and $BD$ intersect at $M$.
questions.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 220" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="300" y2="120" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="104" y1="20" x2="104" y2="200" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1"/><text x="292" y="114" font-size="13" fill="currentColor">x</text><text x="110" y="30" font-size="13" fill="currentColor">y</text><text x="92" y="134" font-size="13" fill="currentColor">O</text><path d="M48 180 L216 140 L272 40 L104 80 Z" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" fill="none"/><line x1="48" y1="180" x2="272" y2="40" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><line x1="216" y1="140" x2="104" y2="80" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><circle cx="48" cy="180" r="2.5" fill="currentColor"/><circle cx="216" cy="140" r="2.5" fill="currentColor"/><circle cx="272" cy="40" r="2.5" fill="currentColor"/><circle cx="104" cy="80" r="2.5" fill="currentColor"/><text x="26" y="198" font-size="13" fill="currentColor">A(-2;-3)</text><text x="222" y="156" font-size="13" fill="currentColor">B(4;-1)</text><text x="260" y="30" font-size="13" fill="currentColor">C(6;4)</text><text x="58" y="74" font-size="13" fill="currentColor">D</text><text x="166" y="102" font-size="13" fill="currentColor">M</text></svg>

parts.q: Determine the coordinates of $M$, the midpoint of $AC$.
If $ABCD$ is a parallelogram, determine the coordinates of $D$.
solution: The diagonals of a parallelogram bisect each other, so $M$ is also the midpoint of $BD$.

parts.q: Prove that $ABCD$ is NOT a rectangle.
solution: $\therefore AB$ is not perpendicular to $BC$, so the interior angles are not $90^{\circ}$ and $ABCD$ is not a rectangle.
parts.answer: $m_{AB}\times m_{BC}=\frac{5}{6}\neq -1$, so adjacent sides are not perpendicular

parts.q: Write down the gradient and the $y$-intercept of $\ell$.
Determine the equation of the line through the point $(3; -1)$ that is perpendicular to $\ell$.
Calculate $\theta$, the angle of inclination of $\ell$, rounded to two decimal places.

questions.context: In the diagram, the circle defined by $x^2+y^2-6x+4y-12=0$ has centre $M$. The point $A(7; 1)$ lies on the circle, and the tangent to the circle at $A$ is drawn.
questions.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 220" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><line x1="20" y1="78" x2="250" y2="78" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="78" y1="14" x2="78" y2="206" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1"/><text x="242" y="72" font-size="13" fill="currentColor">x</text><text x="84" y="24" font-size="13" fill="currentColor">y</text><text x="66" y="92" font-size="13" fill="currentColor">O</text><circle cx="126" cy="110" r="80" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" fill="none"/><line x1="126" y1="110" x2="190" y2="62" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><line x1="166" y1="30" x2="238" y2="126" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5"/><circle cx="126" cy="110" r="2.5" fill="currentColor"/><circle cx="190" cy="62" r="2.5" fill="currentColor"/><text x="96" y="128" font-size="13" fill="currentColor">M</text><text x="196" y="54" font-size="13" fill="currentColor">A(7;1)</text></svg>

parts.q: Determine the coordinates of the centre $M$ and the radius of the circle.
Show that the point $A(7; 1)$ lies on the circle.
Determine the equation of the tangent to the circle at $A$. Write your answer in the form $y=mx+c$.
solution: The tangent is perpendicular to the radius at the point of contact: $m_{tan}=-\frac{4}{3}$

questions.context: The circle with equation $x^2+y^2=20$ has its centre at the origin $O$. The point $T(2; 4)$ lies on the circle. The tangent to the circle at $T$ intersects the $x$-axis at $R$ and the $y$-axis at $S$.

parts.q: Show that $T(2; 4)$ lies on the circle.
solution: $2^2+4^2=4+16=20$, so $T$ satisfies $x^2+y^2=20$ and lies on the circle.

parts.q: Determine the equation of the tangent to the circle at $T$.
Calculate the area of $\triangle ORS$, the triangle formed by the tangent and the two axes.
